text.title: typescript-strict-mode Failure Modes
text.summary: Four migration / sustainment antipatterns the spec must prevent.

antipattern.symptom: Sporadic TypeErrors "Cannot read properties of undefined". Repro requires production data; tests pass.
antipattern.root-cause: Code uses `user!.profile!.address!.city`. Each `!` silently strips a `| null | undefined`; the first actual nullish value down the chain throws.
antipattern.fix: Optional chain + nullish coalescing: `user?.profile?.address?.city ?? '—'`. OR assertion function: `assertDefined(user); …`. Add lint backstop `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion`.

antipattern.symptom: HTTP 500s in production after deploying a new endpoint; the controller receives the request but the service crashes on a missing field.
antipattern.root-cause: `const body = req.body as Order` casts unknown to Order without runtime validation. The body actually shipped missing `amount`.
antipattern.fix: Replace with OrderSchema.parse(req.body) or safeParse. The cast is forbidden by rule r5-no-as-cast-on-unknown.

antipattern.symptom: CI passes but a downstream consumer crashes with TypeError; the strict flag's error was suppressed and reappeared at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: Developer added `// @ts-expect-error: too lazy to fix` above the offending line. Strict flag's compiler error was silenced; the underlying bug remained.
antipattern.fix: Remove the suppression. Apply the canonical fix strategy from migration_steps[].fix_strategy. Ban `@ts-expect-error` and `@ts-ignore` in CI lint.

antipattern.symptom: One PR enables `strict: true`, surfaces 800 errors, sits open for weeks, accumulates conflicts, never merges.
antipattern.root-cause: The team flipped all strict flags at once. Each error class needs a different fix strategy; mixing them blocks review.
antipattern.fix: Migration spec lists one flag per migration_step; each step is one focused PR within the error_budget. The migration order is fixed: strict → noUncheckedIndexedAccess → exactOptionalPropertyTypes.
